title: [Bug]: Load-profile reports can render non-standard JSON values
summary: Synthetic load-profile phase timing objects can carry non-finite values and the JSON report renderer uses default json.dumps behavior, allowing NaN or Infinity in public evidence.
observed: A load-profile phase containing NaN can be represented and rendered as non-standard JSON.
expected: Load-profile timing objects should reject non-finite values before report rendering.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that constructs a load-profile phase with NaN timing data.
impact: Synthetic load reports are used as public release and issue evidence. They should stay standards-compliant and deterministic.
delivery_semantics: No runtime delivery behavior changes. This fix affects local test evidence generation only.
security: The fix must preserve public-safe report output and must not expose private service locators, credentials, payloads, or filesystem details.
acceptance: A focused regression test proves non-finite load-profile phase timings are rejected.
Valid load-profile reports still render as JSON and Markdown.
tests: Run the focused regression test, load-profile tests, synthetic harness tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update CHANGELOG.md and testing documentation to mention strict load-profile evidence output.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published. Include the release tag, regression evidence, verification evidence, and any remaining limitations.
